subject: Cloud Computing
title: Firewalls in the Cloud
description: Learn Firewalls in the Cloud in one minute.

hook.text: In the physical world, a firewall is a wall that stops fire. In the cloud, it stops unauthorized traffic.

buildup.text: Cloud firewalls — security groups and network ACLs — control which traffic can enter or leave your resources.

discovery.text: Rules are defined by IP address, port, and protocol. Allow web traffic on port 443, block everything else.

twist.text: The default rule matters most. AWS security groups deny all inbound by default — Azure NSGs may not.

climax.text: Cloud firewalls are your first line of defense. But they only work if rules are reviewed regularly.

punchline.text: A firewall is only as good as its rules. Review them often.

quiz.question: What do cloud security groups control?
options: Which network traffic is allowed to reach or leave cloud resources
Which users can log into the cloud management console
How much bandwidth each cloud resource can use
